transactions.agency: Florida Department of Health, Florida
transactions.description: Annual WordPress Website Technical Support and Content UpdatesAnnual Technical Support and Content Updates for the web hosting period of October 1, 2020 through September 30, 2021. This is to include the following:1. Install WordFence, Wordfence is a very popular plugin that will help eliminate spam and provides added security.2. Install the Google recaptcha system (https://www.google.com/recaptcha/intro/v3.html) which will help to stop bots communicating through the website.3. Configure the server to block bot traffic and other known spam IP's.4. Administrative Changes to Users and Security Levels5. Database Management6. Content Updates including, but not limited to revising or adding pages, headers, data elements, images, events, meetings, display manners, website navigation/architecture7. This should also include the cost of migrating current website data/files from current server to new server (if applicable) by the end of the current hosting period which is September 30, 2020.

transactions.description: ANNUAL SUBSCRIPTION: PART # D1XWVLL-This Purchase Order is for the Procurement of Red Book Database Flat Files for the Red Book Expanded Extract with weekly updates. This will allow the subscriber to access and download the most current file on a weekly basis from Red Book's secure download site. They provide the URL for the Red Book download site and a user name and password via e-mail that enables a subscriber paying for weekly updates to download the most current file on a weekly basis. This cost also includes 24/7 customer support. Red Book provides the latest pricing and product information to help you quickly answer drug-related questions with information for more than 200,000 prescription and over-the-counter drugs, nutraceuticals, and bulk chemicals, but it also includes a broad spectrum of the healthcare information you need to help you keep pace with today???s changing healthcare environment all in one source. service dates Feb. 11, 2021 through Jan. 11,2022

transactions.description: FY21-22: FDOT Job 6800 Security Manager (3. Senior Manager). (Ken Barnett)FDOT is requesting staff to assist with the review of security logs and analytics tools. This includes:1. Reviewing existing security tools (Microsoft Defender for Endpoints, Splunk, Azure Sentinel, Azure Security Center, and Elastic) for patterns and indicators necessary for incident investigations. 2. Create security-minded correlation reports, alerts, and dashboards in Splunk. 3. Knowledge transfer to FDOT staff on the developed security dashboards and alerts.4. Review log data produced natively by firewalls and end-point detection and response tools for security indicators that support incident investigations. 5. Providing intel/recommendations based on monitoring and reviews.

transactions.description: FY22-23: SIM Project FDOT Job 6800 Security Manager (3. Senior Manager) (Dakota Sams) FDOT is requesting staff to assist with the review of security logs and analytics tools. This includes: 1. Reviewing existing security tools (Microsoft Defender for Endpoints, Splunk, Azure Sentinel, Azure Security Center, and Elastic) for patterns and indicators necessary for incident investigations. 2. Create security-minded correlation reports, alerts, and dashboards in Splunk. 3. Knowledge transfer to FDOT staff on the developed security dashboards and alerts. 4. Review log data produced natively by firewalls and end-point detection and response tools for security indicators that support incident investigations. 5. Providing intel/recommendations based on monitoring and reviews.

transactions.agency: Florida Department of Transportation, Florida
transactions.description: FY22-23: SIM Project FDOT Job 6800 Security Manager (3. Senior Manager) (Dakota Sams) FDOT is requesting staff to assist with the review of security logs and analytics tools. This includes: 1. Reviewing existing security tools (Microsoft Defender for Endpoints, Splunk, Azure Sentinel, Azure Security Center, and Elastic) for patterns and indicators necessary for incident investigations. 2. Create security-minded correlation reports, alerts, and dashboards in Splunk. 3. Knowledge transfer to FDOT staff on the developed security dashboards and alerts. 4. Review log data produced natively by firewalls and end-point detection and response tools for security indicators that support incident investigations. 5. Providing intel/recommendations based on monitoring and reviews.

transactions.agency: Florida Department of Transportation, Florida
transactions.description: FY22-23: SIM Project FDOT Job 6800 Security Manager (3. Senior Manager) (Dakota Sams) FDOT is requesting staff to assist with the review of security logs and analytics tools. This includes: 1. Reviewing existing security tools (Microsoft Defender for Endpoints, Splunk, Azure Sentinel, Azure Security Center, and Elastic) for patterns and indicators necessary for incident investigations. 2. Create security-minded correlation reports, alerts, and dashboards in Splunk. 3. Knowledge transfer to FDOT staff on the developed security dashboards and alerts. 4. Review log data produced natively by firewalls and end-point detection and response tools for security indicators that support incident investigations. 5. Providing intel/recommendations based on monitoring and reviews.

transactions.description: Management consulting service to provide recommendations for implementing a comprehensive programmatic monitoring system that will measure true program and service delivery quality and effectiveness from a critical thinking perspective, moving beyond a compliance-based approach as per the requirements as stipulated in the attached RFQ #DCF RFQ 2122 089. The Department?s Quality Office conducts programmatic monitoring activities as outlined in Section 409.997(2(b), F.S. This statute specifically requires regular and periodic monitoring activities that track the identified outcome measures on a statewide, regional, and provider-specific basis. Line item 7 deliverable represents: Final Summary Report, including asset inventory and recommended Department implementation plan. Deliverable due 10/15/2022.

transactions.agency: Florida Department of Transportation, Florida
transactions.description: FY22-23 FDOT- Job 1430 Database Analyst C. Advanced (Working Title ? Data Integration Specialist) (Anup Jawale) The candidate in this position will be responsible for assisting in the data analysis, data quality and data integration activities of FDOT?s legacy data sources. The candidate must be skilled in data analysis of legacy data including DB2/zOS, Oracle and SQL Server database platforms as well as modern data platforms including Azure SQL DB. The candidate must be able to understand and demonstrate the ability to develop data quality and integration processes utilizing modern data integration tools. The candidate must be able to work as part of a team to supports the Department?s data needs.

transactions.description: FY23-24: SRM Project Job 6800 Security Management (Security Manager) (Dakota Sams) FDOT is requesting staff to assist with the review of security logs and analytics tools. This includes: 1. Reviewing existing security tools (Microsoft Defender for Endpoints, Splunk, Azure Sentinel, Azure Security Center, and Elastic) for patterns and indicators necessary for incident investigations. 2. Create security-minded correlation reports, alerts, and dashboards in Splunk. 3. Knowledge transfer to FDOT staff on the developed security dashboards and alerts. 4. Review log data produced natively by firewalls and end-point detection and response tools for security indicators that support incident investigations. 5. Providing intel/recommendations based on monitoring and reviews.

transactions.description: FY24-25: SRM Project Job 6800 Security Manager 3. Sr. Manager (Dakota Sams) The SOC will assist with the review of security logs and analytics tools. This includes: 1. Reviewing existing security tools (Microsoft Defender for Endpoints, Splunk, Azure Sentinel, Azure Security Center, and Elastic) for patterns and indicators necessary for incident investigations. 2. Create security-minded correlation reports, alerts, and dashboards in Splunk. 3. Knowledge transfer to FDOT staff on the developed security dashboards and alerts. 4. Review log data produced natively by firewalls and end-point detection and response tools for security indicators that support incident investigations. 5. Providing intel/recommendations based on monitoring and reviews.

transactions.description: WittyAI is a trainable AI chatbot solution that will enable consumers to obtain answers swiftly and accurately, thereby potentially reducing the volume of calls directed to the call center. This unique proprietary software utilizes three independent service providers: AWS server hosting; Pinecone vector database integration and hosting; and OpenAI API integration. All work and associated costs are described in the attached SOW, dated June 3, 2024. Monthly charges to include: The WittyAI platform instance to be billed at $2500 per month for up to 3 instances. Utilization service fees from 3 independent providers - 1) OpenAI (https://openai.com/api/pricing/) - Model: gpt-40; Input: $5.00 / 1M tokens; Output: $15.00 / 1M tokens 2) Pinecone.io (https://www.pinecone.io/pricing/) - Standard us-east-1; Storage (GB/Month) $0.00045; Read Units ($/1M RU): $8.25000; Write Units ($/1M WU): $2.00000 3) Amazon Web Services (https://aws.amazon.com/ec2/pricing/on-demand/) - Instance Name: t2.med

This is to be followed by weekly, 30-minute meetings to discuss status updates on the Provider???s work in completing and meeting the tasks, deliverables, and performance measures for all campaign materials. The Provider shall be available to coordinate and attend all meetings.

transactions.agency: Florida Department of Children and Families, Florida
transactions.description: Submit the final draft of the CPI and APS campaign name, slogan, logo, toolkit, and style guide. Submit final draft of communications strategy. The Provider shall submit the final draft of the CPI campaign and APS campaign materials, including the campaign name, slogan, logo, toolkit, and style guide within 50 days after initial kick off meeting. Failure to create the final campaign name, slogan, logo, toolkit, and style guide and submit the final draft of the communications strategy, will result in a deduction of 5% of the total deliverable costs beyond the due date. Such deduction shall be made from the deliverable payment.

transactions.agency: Florida Department of Children and Families, Florida
transactions.description: Submit the second draft of the CPI campaign and APS campaign materials, including the campaign name, slogan, and logo. Submit first draft of the toolkit, style guide, and communications strategy The Provider shall submit the second draft of the CPI campaign and APS campaign materials, including the campaign name, slogan, and logo, in accordance with the agreed upon timeline. The Provider shall submit the first draft of the toolkit, style guide, and communications strategy in accordance with the agreed upon timeline. Failure to submit the second draft of the campaign materials and the first draft of the messaging toolkit and style guide, will result in a deduction of 5% of the total deliverable costs beyond the respective due dates. Such deduction shall be made from the deliverable payment

The D-SNAP processes will require dedicated operational and technical support staff to support and modify/enhance the D-SNAP and FLORIDA Systems and to support DCF, in concurrence the Federal Government taking steps to assist residents of areas who are affected by HURRICANE HELENE allowing them to apply for and receive Disaster Food Assistance (Disaster Supplemental Nutritional Assistance Program ??? D-SNAP). and as set forth by attached SOW The ACCESS Florida system includes the FLORIDA, the D-SNAP and the D-SNAP Volunteer Systems that support the collection and processing of D-SNAP applications. transactions.description: WittyAI is a trainable AI chatbot solution that will enable consumers to obtain answers swiftly and accurately, thereby potentially reducing the volume of calls directed to the call center. transactions.description: WittyAI is a trainable AI chatbot solution that will enable consumers to obtain answers swiftly and accurately, thereby potentially reducing the volume of calls directed to the call center. This unique proprietary software utilizes three independent service providers: AWS server hosting; Pinecone vector database integration and hosting; and OpenAI API integration. All work and associated costs are described in the attached SOW, dated February 17, 2025. : The WittyAI platform instance to be billed annually at $30,000 for up to 5 instances. The PO is pro-rated for 2 months of platform support May 1 - June 30, 2025. Service period: May 1, 2025 - June 30, 2025

transactions.description: FY25-26: SRM Project Job 6800 Security Management - Security Manager (Level 3) (Dakota Sams) FDOT is requesting staff to assist with the review of security logs and analytics tools. This includes: 1. Reviewing existing security tools (Microsoft Defender for Endpoints, Splunk, Azure Sentinel, Azure Security Center, and Elastic) for patterns and indicators necessary for incident investigations. 2. Create security-minded correlation reports, alerts, and dashboards in Splunk. 3. Knowledge transfer to FDOT staff on the developed security dashboards and alerts. 4. Review log data produced natively by firewalls and end-point detection and response tools for security indicators that support incident investigations. Providing intel/recommendations based on monitoring and reviews.

transactions.description: FY24-25: SRM Project Job 6800 Security Manager 3. Sr. Manager (Dakota Sams) The SOC will assist with the review of security logs and analytics tools. This includes: 1. Reviewing existing security tools (Microsoft Defender for Endpoints, Splunk, Azure Sentinel, Azure Security Center, and Elastic) for patterns and indicators necessary for incident investigations. 2. Create security-minded correlation reports, alerts, and dashboards in Splunk. 3. Knowledge transfer to FDOT staff on the developed security dashboards and alerts. 4. Review log data produced natively by firewalls and end-point detection and response tools for security indicators that support incident investigations. 5. Providing intel/recommendations based on monitoring and reviews.
